evals.prompt: I need to set up a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically uploads our AAB to the internal track whenever we push a tag like v1.2.3. Our app is com.widgetco.app and we already have the service account JSON stored as a GitHub secret called PLAY_SA_KEY. Can you write the workflow file?
evals.expected_output: Complete GitHub Actions workflow YAML that triggers on tag push, installs GPC, and uploads to internal track
expectations: Triggers on push tags v* pattern
Sets GPC_SERVICE_ACCOUNT from the PLAY_SA_KEY secret
Installs GPC with npm install -g gpc
Runs gpc releases upload or gpc publish with --track internal

evals.prompt: Our CI pipeline keeps failing intermittently with NETWORK_ERROR when uploading to Play Store. The AAB is about 80MB and we're on a shared CI runner. Sometimes it works, sometimes it times out. How can I make this more reliable?
evals.expected_output: Configures retry and timeout environment variables to handle transient network failures
expectations: Mentions GPC_BASE_DELAY and GPC_MAX_DELAY for backoff tuning
Shows the env vars in a CI YAML format

evals.prompt: We want a full release pipeline: upload to beta on tag push, then a manual workflow_dispatch to promote from beta to production with a staged rollout. The promotion step should check vitals first and only promote if crash rate is under 2%. We also want the release status written to the GitHub step summary.
evals.expected_output: Two GitHub Actions workflows: auto-upload on tag and manual promote with vitals gating and step summary
expectations: Provides two workflow files or a single file with two jobs
First workflow triggers on tag push and uploads to beta
Second workflow uses workflow_dispatch for manual promotion
Includes gpc vitals crashes --threshold 2.0 before promotion
Uses gpc releases promote --from beta --to production with rollout percentage
